title: Puerto Banus No Speedboat Hire Licence and Costs in 2026
html:
Puerto Banus No offers direct access to the Mediterranean, with speedboat hire available from the main jetty near the port entrance. Most operators let you hire a boat without a licence if you stick to daylight hours and stay within the bay, with prices ranging from 120 to 400 EUR for a half-day trip. BoatHire24 lists verified options that suit both licence-free and licensed hire.

Do you need a licence to rent a speedboat in Puerto Banus No?

No licence is required for most speedboats under 15 HP on the Spanish coast, but many rental companies require a valid boating licence for larger engines. For boats above 15 HP, a Patron de Navegacion Basica or an International Certificate of Competence is typically needed. The operator will confirm exact licence requirements when you book.

How much does it cost to rent a speedboat in Puerto Banus No?

Speedboat hire in Puerto Banus No starts at around 120 EUR for a two-hour tour and can reach 650 EUR for a full day on a mid-range model. Peak summer months from June to September see higher rates, while shoulder seasons offer discounts of up to 20 percent. You can compare prices and hire a boat directly through the marketplace.

Where to set off in Puerto Banus No

Most rentals depart from Puerto Banus Marina, the main hub with fuel dock and parking. Other common launch points include the public slipway near the fishing port and the jetty at the western end of the beach. Popular destinations within a 15-nautical-mile radius include the shores of Marbella, the quiet coves at Cabopino, and the sandbanks off Estepona. For longer trips, boaters often head toward the Balearic Islands, though that requires an overnight stop.

Which boat to rent

  1. QuickSilver Activ 605 Open, ideal for coastal cruising with up to six passengers, from 250 EUR half-day. See this speedboat listing for details.
  2. Axopar 22 Sun Top, a versatile day cruiser for up to seven people, from 300 EUR half-day.
  3. Fairline Phantom 34, a cabin motorboat for two couples, from 500 EUR full day.
  4. Sunseeker Predator 57, a luxury express yacht for up to ten guests, from 1,200 EUR full day.
  5. Rib tender, a small rigid inflatable for up to four, from 120 EUR for two hours.

Practical costs for speedboat hire

  • Shared tour (2 hours): 50, 80 EUR per person
  • Small motorboat half-day (4 hours): 120, 250 EUR for the boat
  • Catamaran full-day (8 hours): 600, 1,200 EUR for up to 12 guests
  • Crewed yacht full-day: from 800 EUR for a 30-foot vessel
Tip: Book through a reputable platform like BoatHire24 to ensure the vessel is insured and meets safety standards. Always check the weather forecast for the Poniente wind, which can pick up quickly in summer.

At a glance

  • Price band: 120, 1,200 EUR per day
  • Peak months: June, July, August, September
  • Trip length: 2 hours, half-day, or full-day
  • Capacity: 4 to 12 people
  • Currency: EUR

Questions about boat rental in Puerto Banus No

Can I rent a boat without a licence in Puerto Banus No?

Yes, you can rent a small speedboat under 15 HP without a licence, provided you stay within the bay and operate during daylight hours.

What is the best time of year to rent a speedboat in Puerto Banus No?

The best time is from late May to early October, when sea conditions are calm and temperatures are warm, with July and August being the hottest months.

Are there any age restrictions for boating in Puerto Banus No?

Drivers must be at least 18 years old with a valid ID; some operators set a minimum age of 21 for larger vessels.

How far can I take a no-licence speedboat from Puerto Banus No?

No-licence rentals typically limit you to a maximum of 3 nautical miles from the marina, which is enough to reach Marbella Bay and nearby coves.

Written by the BoatHire24 team, who book and list verified boats in Puerto Banus No and hundreds of destinations worldwide.
